title: White Sauce – specially for Gert Smit
excerpt: 125 ml (half a cup) cake flour 125 ml (half a cup) butter/ margarine salt and pepper to taste 250 ml milk – more milk if you prefer a thinner sauce 125 ml grated cheese NOW melt the butter/margarine in a pot on the stove. with your whisk stir the flour into the butter …

seo.title: White Sauce - specially for Gert Smit - Marinda Kook

html:
• 125 ml (half a cup) cake flour
  • 125 ml (half a cup) butter/ margarine
  • salt and pepper to taste
  • 250 ml milk – more milk if you prefer a thinner sauce
  • 125 ml grated cheese

NOW

  1. melt the butter/margarine in a pot on the stove.
  2. with your whisk stir the flour into the butter
  3. add salt and pepper to taste. You can add a bit of cayenne pepper yourself for some kick
  4. now start stirring the milk in slowly with your whisk. It depends on how runny you want it. That determines your quantity of milk. Let it simmer gently otherwise you'll get a “raw taste.” I'd say about 6 minutes – just keep stirring.
  5. then right at the end stir in cheese to taste

The sauce can be used over cabbage, on boiled potatoes or over pancakes. Gert, I hope this helps.
